Text: We do not need a constitutional amendment for children to pray, and what I did was ask the Department of Justice and the Department of Education to issue, for the first time, a set of guidelines that we gave to every school in America, stating that children could not be prevented from engaging in religious activity when they prayed, when they did everything they could do according to the constitution, simply because they were on public school grounds, and I think anyone who has experienced it will tell you that our administration has done more than any other in years to clarify religious freedom in public spaces, including in public schools.
